replication_study.hypothesis: Mean aversiveness ratings for Covid-19 verbal jokes will be higher than mean aversiveness ratings for non-Covid verbal jokes (i.e., βCovid > 0 when non-Covid is the reference category).

data_plan.source_type: Online survey (Qualtrics/formr) of U.S. residents recruited via Prolific
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single data-collection wave (Jan 19–Feb 2021) split across two survey halves
data_plan.sample_size: ≈ 1,324 unique participants; > 250 k individual joke ratings after reshaping to long format
data_plan.access_details: Provided as de-identified CSV files in /app/data/original/21/input/replication_data; unrestricted academic use

qualification.explanation: The dataset was collected independently from the original Italian study but with the same stimulus set, rating scales, and survey logic, allowing a direct test of the focal Covid-19-aversiveness effect on a new population.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Stimuli, rating scales (0–6 for aversiveness), and variable names match those used by Bischetti et al. (see mapping steps in Analysis_updated.Rmd lines showing label levels covid-verbal vs non-verbal). The focal comparison Covid Verbal (M = 0.87) vs Non Covid Verbal (M = 0.48) reported in the paper is identifiable through the same ‘label’ factor once the meta-data mapping is merged (Analysis_updated.Rmd).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original sample: 1,903 Italian residents surveyed 18–30 Mar 2020 during national lockdown (post_registration.json, original_paper.pdf). New sample: ≈ 1,324 U.S. residents surveyed Jan 2021 (state column with U.S. state abbreviations; filter dates in Analysis_updated.Rmd). Cultural context, pandemic phase, language, and timing differ, providing an independent replication while holding materials constant.

data_plan.notes: Birth year stored in ‘age’ column (1920–2002) needs conversion to age (2021 – birth_year). Item-to-type mapping is loaded from missing Method XLSX files; if unavailable, labels can be recovered from the column-name prefixes (e.g., covid text = pics 1–99).

steps: Load Part1 and Part2 de-identified CSVs with rio::import().
Merge on participant_id (all = TRUE) to retain every recorded rating.
Select aversiveness variables (*_disturbing), participant_id, item name, age, state.
Reshape wide-to-long (reshape2::melt) to obtain one row per rating.
Subtract 1 from aversiveness scores to convert to 0–6 scale (Analysis_updated.Rmd).
Merge with item-type meta-data to create factor ‘label’ with levels: covid-verbal, covid-meme, covid-strip, non-verbal.
Filter data to the two verbal conditions (covid-verbal vs non-verbal).
Fit a linear mixed-effects model: Aversiveness ~ CovidIndicator + (1|participant_id) + (1|item).
Extract fixed-effect estimate (βCovid) and its t-test (lmerTest).
Compute descriptive means and 95 % CIs for both conditions.
planned_method.models: Linear Mixed-Effects Model (LMM) with random intercepts for participants and items; non-Covid verbal is reference category.
planned_method.independent_variables: CovidIndicator (1 = Covid-19 verbal joke, 0 = non-Covid verbal joke)
planned_method.control_variables: None for focal test (age/state optional robustness checks)
planned_method.tools_software: R (≥ 4.2) with packages: rio, reshape2, lme4, lmerTest, emmeans

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Fixed-effect coefficient βCovid representing mean difference in aversiveness
planned_estimation_and_test.test: t-test on βCovid provided by lmerTest (Satterthwaite df)

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion of rows with NA in outcome, predictor, or grouping factors.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Statistical significance at α = 0.05, two-tailed; direction must be positive to confirm hypothesis.

files.replicate_analysis.R: Self-contained R script executing the steps listed above; reads CSVs from /app/data, produces model summaries and descriptive statistics.
files.Analysis_updated.Rmd (existing): Original processing notebook from U.S. data collection; serves as reference for variable cleaning and factor coding.

codebase.notes: The new script will borrow cleaning code blocks from Analysis_updated.Rmd but restrict analysis to verbal jokes. All output (tables, .RDS) will be written into /app/data.

analysis.instructions: Run `Rscript replicate_analysis.R` inside the container. The script will load and clean the data, fit the LMM, and save a results summary to /app/data/replication_results.txt.
analysis.comparison_metrics: 1) Sign of βCovid; 2) Magnitude of βCovid vs original reported +0.39; 3) p-value (<0.05); 4) Condition means (Covid-verbal vs non-Covid-verbal).
